scope: Decision record only. No SARIF generation, upload, workflow integration, code-scanning enablement, required-check change, or alert ownership change is performed by #269.

evidence_inputs.decision_relevance: Baseline and suppression governance is valid for current fixture/custom-check evidence, but there is no live analyzer artifact to upload as SARIF.
Engine evidence remains split. PowerShell 7 PSScriptAnalyzer and Windows PowerShell 5.1 runtime evidence are future or unclaimed, so code-scanning alerts would overstate current proof.
The review-assist JSON and Markdown artifacts are sufficient for current reviewer handoff and explicitly do not claim SARIF, code scanning, workflow, or required-check readiness.

current_series_review_path.rationale: The current JSON/Markdown artifacts expose source reports, normalized findings, inventory context, baseline and suppression state, engine/Pester boundaries, missing optional analyzer evidence, unclaimed artifacts, warnings, and explicit non-claims without creating GitHub security alerts.

defer_rationale: No validated committed live PSScriptAnalyzer report is present for #262.
The #268 report is explicitly local committed JSON/Markdown evidence and does not claim SARIF generation or upload readiness.
The #267 boundary leaves PowerShell 7 analyzer evidence and Windows PowerShell 5.1 runtime evidence as unclaimed or future evidence.

Uploading current fixture/custom-check evidence as code-scanning alerts would risk noisy or misleading alert ownership before fingerprints, severity mapping, event restrictions, and alert lifecycle are designed.
Parent #260 can continue through workflow/local integration planning using fail-closed JSON/Markdown report generation without requiring SARIF.
future_sarif_implementation_requirements: Separate current-session operator approval for SARIF implementation and workflow mutation.
SARIF producer definition, including source reports consumed and whether fixture evidence, live analyzer evidence, or both are represented.
SARIF schema/version compatibility proof, including SARIF 2.1.0 output validation.
Stable rule IDs, rule metadata, severity-to-SARIF-level mapping, and help/fix text.
Stable fingerprints or partial fingerprints that prevent alert churn across regenerated reports.
Artifact names and readback path for generated SARIF before upload.
Minimal workflow permissions, including any security-events: write requirement and why the event context is safe.
Pull request fork/private-repo behavior and explicit pull_request_target avoidance or justification.
Code-scanning alert ownership, triage expectations, and noise-control policy.
Upload run evidence and separate readback of actual code-scanning alerts before claiming alert quality.
non_claims: No SARIF file is generated by #269.
No SARIF upload is performed by #269.
No GitHub workflow file is changed by #269.
No code-scanning alert, code-scanning enablement, or required-check behavior is changed by #269.
No pull_request_target workflow design is introduced by #269.
No artifact-retention setting is changed by #269.
No live PSScriptAnalyzer evidence is claimed when the #262 analyzer report is absent.
No parent #260 closeability or #270 workflow-readiness claim is made by #269.

handoff_to_270.notes: Because SARIF upload is deferred, #270 should plan workflow/local integration around the existing fail-closed JSON/Markdown report path unless a future approval changes this decision.

warnings: SARIF upload is deferred, not rejected forever; later implementation requires a fresh explicit mutation gate and evidence readback.
